documentation.readme: Purpose Villain is a high-level Stage 0/1 C2 framework that can handle multiple reverse TCP and HoaxShell-based shells, enhance their functionality with additional features (commands, utilities), and share them among connected sibling servers (Villain instances running on different machines).
usage_sections: Installation: Villain has been explicitly developed and tested on kali linux. You can install it with apt: ❗New releases may take time to be incorporated into kali's repositories. For the latest version or if you prefer to install it manually: You must also install gnome-terminal (required for

Usage: You should run as root: Check out the Usage Guide for more. :warning: Create your own obfuscated reverse shell templates and replace the default ones in your instance of Villain to better handle AV evasion. Here's how 📽️ -> youtube.com/watch?v=grSBdZdUya0

arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Purpose Villain is a high-level Stage 0/1 C2 framework that can handle multiple reverse TCP and HoaxShell-based shells, enhance their functionality with additional features (commands, utilities), and share them among connected sibling servers (Villain instances running on different machines).
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: apt install villain; git clone https://github.com/t3l3machus/Villain. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd ./Villain`.
